page: Amazon Web Services c6a.12xlarge is a compute-optimized server instance powered by the AWS Nitro hypervisor. It features an x86_64 architecture with an AMD EPYC 7R13 processor operating at 3.6 GHz. The configuration provides 48 vCPUs across 24 physical cores with hyperthreading enabled, delivering dedicated CPU allocation. It is equipped with 96.0 GB of DDR4 memory running at 3 GHz, translating to 2.0 GB of RAM per core. This instance does not include local storage or GPU hardware, and it provides a baseline network bandwidth of 19 Gbps.

Benchmark data indicates strong CPU capabilities, with stress-ng single-core and multi-core performance ranking in the top 25%. Passmark CPU and encryption tests, along with Geekbench ray tracing and image processing, also place in the top 25%. However, memory bandwidth for both cached and uncached reads and writes remains in the middle 50%, as do software compilation, database operations, and Redis SET operations. Text generation for small LLM models is strong, while prompt processing and larger model inference perform in the average tier.

The instance presents a clear tradeoff by omitting local NVMe storage and GPU accelerators, which reduces hardware complexity and aligns cost efficiency toward pure CPU-bound tasks. The 2:1 memory-to-vCPU ratio makes it highly efficient for compute-intensive workloads that do not require massive memory footprints. Appropriate workloads include batch processing, distributed analytics, image processing, ray tracing, and CPU-based machine learning inference.
description: Amazon Web Services c6a.12xlarge is an x86_64 compute-optimized server instance running on the Nitro hypervisor. Powered by a dedicated 3.6 GHz AMD EPYC 7R13 processor, it features 48 vCPUs, 24 physical cores, and 96.0 GB of DDR4 memory. The instance lacks local storage and GPU accelerators, focusing resources entirely on compute density. Benchmarks show strong top-25% performance in multi-core CPU execution, encryption, ray tracing, and image processing, while memory bandwidth and database operations perform in the average tier. With a baseline network bandwidth of 19 Gbps, this instance offers a cost-effective profile for CPU-bound workloads. It is highly suitable for batch processing, ray tracing, image processing, and small-scale machine learning inference tasks that require dedicated compute resources without the overhead of local storage.
og_description: Amazon Web Services c6a.12xlarge is a compute-optimized instance featuring 48 vCPUs, 96 GB DDR4 RAM, and an AMD EPYC 7R13 processor, delivering strong multi-core and encryption performance.
meta_description: Amazon Web Services c6a.12xlarge is an x86_64 compute-optimized instance with 48 vCPUs, 96 GB RAM, and a 3.6 GHz AMD EPYC processor.
tagline: A dedicated compute-optimized instance featuring forty-eight vCPUs and ninety-six gigabytes of memory for CPU-intensive processing workloads.
bullet_points: Dedicated AMD EPYC 7R13 processor with 48 vCPUs and 24 physical cores
96.0 GB of DDR4 memory providing a 2:1 memory-to-vCPU ratio
Strong top-25% performance in multi-core CPU, encryption, and ray tracing benchmarks
19 Gbps baseline network bandwidth powered by the AWS Nitro hypervisor
Designed for CPU-bound workloads such as batch processing and image rendering
